lenses.guidance: vv 30-35's altar-on-Ebal and Torah-reading enacts the Deut 27 command verbatim — Joshua does what Moses prescribed. The canonical thread connects Sinai code to Shechem ceremony; obedience completes the covenant.

vv 1-29's ambush narrative (military) and vv 30-35's covenant ceremony (liturgical) are paired in a single chapter. The structural juxtaposition makes conquest and worship one act; victory's frame is the altar, not the sword.

vv 30-35's Ebal-Gerizim ceremony reactivates the Deuteronomic covenant on conquered ground — promise embedded into landscape. The redemption arc is now spatially located; covenant has a place, not just a people.
